title: Microcoelia africana
taxonomy: Microcoelia africana belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is classified within the genus Microcoelia. The genus Microcoelia is a part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. This species, Microcoelia africana, is a type of mycoheterotrophic orchid, meaning it obtains its nutrients by parasitizing fungi rather than through photosynthesis. Microcoelia africana is native to tropical regions of Africa. This classification places the species within the broader category of flowering plants.

benefits: Microcoelia africana, also known as the "stool sac fungus," is a parasitic organism that forms a unique relationship with certain species of orchids.
